WARNING: A2338 (820-02020) MBP 13" M1 ONLY. Has Touch Bar, fan, 2x USB-C, no MagSafe. SVR AON is 36A.
data_source: Schematic PNGs cross-referenced with saf-diagnostics-2026-03-02.json (22 logic board cases). Verified against Eason (iphone_cpu_fix) M1 standby circuit reference.

forced_start_bypass.description: Eason diagnostic shortcut: bypass protective isolation to isolate fault location
forced_start_bypass.when_to_use: Board draws 0A or very low current at 20V. Unsure if fault is pre-SVR (charger/CD3217) or post-SVR (PMU/SoC).
steps: 1. Connect PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN to 3.8V externally (4.3V if display attached)
2. Jumper U8110 pins 4 and 5 to force PMU_VDD_HI high
3. Press start button OR connect charger for 3 seconds
forced_start_bypass.if_boots: Fault is FRONT END — CD3217 (UF400/UF500), charger IC (U5200), fuses (FF200/FF201), SPI ROM, boost circuit
forced_start_bypass.if_no_boot: Fault is BACK END — PMU (U8100/U7700), SoC (U0600), clocks (Y0600/Y8301), ROM, NAND

fault_signatures.symptom: No power — 0A at DCPS, no LED, no fan
fault_signatures.likely_cause: CD3217 (UF400/UF500) failure, FF200/FF201 blown fuse, or connector damage
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1016 at 5V/0.023A — heavy liquid damage, CD chips repair + touch bar connector. BM 1234 at 5V — 'not on and working, logic board has liquid damage'. CD3217 failure is a top cause on A2338.
check: CD3217 controllers: UF400 (ATC0 page 57), UF500 (ATC1 page 58)
J5150 SOLDERED battery connector (page 21) — can crack

fault_signatures.symptom: Low current at 5V (0.02-0.2A) — board not negotiating PD
fault_signatures.likely_cause: Charger IC (U5200) fault or liquid damage on charger path
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1016 at 5V/0.023A — liquid damage, 'clan and repair all liquid damage parts touch bar connector CD chips'. BM 1114 at 5V/0.2A — 'very badly liquid damage' requiring CPU swap. Low 5V current on A2338 strongly correlates with liquid damage.
check: U5200 charger IC (353S01525, page 22) — check gate drives PS5211-PS5219
R5275 sense resistor, XW5260 current sense (page 22)
Board must be CLEANED first if liquid damage suspected — ultrasonic + IPA

fault_signatures.symptom: PPBUS_AON present but no PP3V8_AON — 0.3-0.8A at 20V
fault_signatures.likely_cause: Short on PP3V8_AON distribution or U5700 SVR AON failure (36A)
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 940 — 'one ic explosion on logic board (u8250)' near PP3V8_AON area. BM 1166 at 20V/0.8A — short circuit detected. Component explosion is a documented A2338 failure mode.
check: Thermal cam at low voltage — 36A design, check for hot spots

fault_signatures.likely_cause: PMU not cascading, or EDP display line fault
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1166 at 20V/0.8A — 'one LCD line short (EDP from CPU) after T2 swap macbook working fine'. Short on EDP line from CPU can present as partial boot with current stuck at 0.8A.
check: Check PMU outputs (SPMU page 28, MPMU page 32)
Check display connector JP600 (page 68) for damage/corrosion
Check EDP lines from SoC to display — SAF: LCD line short is a known A2338 fault
Try external display to isolate board vs panel
If EDP short confirmed: T2/SoC area rework may be needed

fault_signatures.symptom: Board boots normally (2.0-2.5A at 20V) — keyboard dead
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: 14 of 22 cases (64%) = keyboard fault. BM 1261 at 20V/2.5A — keyboard + backlight. BM 1335 at 20V/2.5A — keyboard + trackpad liquid damage. BM 1144 — Chinese keyboard replacement. Keyboard is the DOMINANT fault on A2338. Shared A2289/A2338 backlight part.
check: Keyboard IOX1 UT101 (PCA6416A) and IOX2 UT102 (page 81)

fault_signatures.likely_cause: DFR connector liquid damage or DFR power rail failure
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1016 — 'touch bar connector has liquid damage, we repair new connector'. BM 1209 — 'touch bar connector has liquid damage, we repair new connector and some liquid damage parts change on logic board'. Touch Bar connector is an A2338-specific liquid damage hotspot.
check: DFR connectors JT700/JT710 (page 86) — inspect for liquid damage
SPI_DFR signals via level shifters UT820/UT830/UT860/UT880/UT890 (page 87)

fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: 9 of 22 cases (41%) = liquid damage keyword. BM 1114 at 5V/0.2A — 'very badly liquid damage' required CPU swap (the most extreme repair). BM 1173 — 'repair logic board liquid damage all parts and ic, found near fan connector Two ic has issue'. BM 1276 at 20V/2.5A — 'near hall sensor ic has liquid damage, make one jumper'. BM 1350 — 'logic board has liquid damage and repair logic board and replace keys'. Liquid damage is MORE prevalent on A2338 (41%) than A2337 (13%).
check: Visual inspection under microscope — focus on Touch Bar connector area (SAF hotspot)
Clean board with IPA + ultrasonic BEFORE diagnosis
Check fan connector area (page 52) — SAF: ICs near fan connector fail
Check hall sensor area — SAF: liquid damage near hall sensor documented

fault_signatures.dcps_reading: Varies — board may still work after repair
fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 940 — 'one ic explosion on logic board (u8250) after we replace ic but still same macbook not taking AMM but on and working'. After replacing U8250 + two CD chips, board fixed. IC explosion is a documented failure mode — visual inspection critical.
check: Identify the failed IC by location (U8250 is near MPMU area)
After replacing visible damage: check for secondary damage from the failure
Check surrounding passives for thermal damage
Full power-on diagnostic after repair to verify no other failures

fault_signatures.saf_data: SAF: BM 1234 — 'try to repair audio ic other parts logic board but still same no improve on sound. No point to spent more time'. Audio IC failure on A2338 can be UNREPAIRABLE — assess cost/benefit before attempting.
check: Speaker amps A/B left (UR600 area), D/E right (UR700 area) — pages 76-77
Speaker connectors JR800 left, JR850 right (page 78)
NOTE: SAF data shows audio IC repair may not be successful — consider board replacement cost
